About the Lab

Sound and Perception Research for Built Environments

SONORIS Lab — from the Latin sonoris, meaning “resonant” — brings together acoustics, psychoacoustics, soundscape, and architectural research under a cohesive interdisciplinary identity.

Based at the NUS Department of Architecture, our work advances understanding of how sound shapes human experience in high-density urban environments, bridging laboratory findings with built outcomes through fundamental perception science, environmental quality assessment, and design translation.

This lab reflects our ambition to serve as a leading platform for interdisciplinary research, design translation, and policy impact. We engage strategically with government agencies (e.g., HDB, MOE, NRF, MND) to advance national R&D priorities under Singapore's City of Tomorrow, Education Research Framework, and Green Built Environment initiatives — translating acoustic science into healthier, more liveable urban environments.

Acoustics & Psychoacoustics (Placeholder Write-up)

Rigorous investigation of how people perceive, evaluate, and respond to sound in architectural and urban contexts.

Soundscape Design (Placeholder Write-up)

Human-centric frameworks for shaping auditory environments that support health, comfort, and community.

Architectural Systems (Placeholder Write-up)

Material, spatial, and technical strategies that translate acoustic evidence into design practice and policy.

Research Training (Placeholder Write-up)

A strong platform for graduate students and early-career researchers to develop internationally competitive expertise.

HDBMOENRFMND

Noise maps L10(18h) at 2 m above ground for 30 HDB precincts.

Principal Investigator

A/Professor Lau Siu-Kit Eddie

Research Focus

Five Themes,
One Mission

01

Architectural and Building Acoustics

Investigating room acoustics, noise isolation, and acoustic material performance in residential, educational, and civic buildings across Singapore's high-density urban fabric (Placeholder Write-up)

02

Soundscape and Perception Modelling

Developing predictive models of how urban residents perceive and evaluate their acoustic environments, drawing on psychoacoustic measurement and ISO 12913 soundscape frameworks (Placeholder Write-up)

03

Acoustic Ventilation Windows and Façade Design

Designing and testing façade and window systems that simultaneously attenuate external noise and enable natural ventilation — critical for tropical high-rise housing typologies (Placeholder Write-up)

04

Psychoacoustic and VR-Based Testing

Applying virtual reality and controlled listening experiments to study how people perceive and respond to complex acoustic environments, enabling rigorous evidence-based design evaluation (Placeholder Write-up)

05

Environmental Noise Policy and Mitigation

Translating acoustic research into evidence-based noise standards and mitigation strategies, informing planning guidelines and policy frameworks for Singapore's built environment agencies (Placeholder Write-up)

Research Projects

Active Investigations

All Projects ↗
NRF / MND / NParks · City of Tomorrow

Predicting Annoyance Responses: Mapping the Influence of Green Infrastructure

Developing predictive models for how urban green infrastructure shapes residents' acoustic annoyance, combining field measurement, machine learning, and VR-based perceptual studies across Singapore's high-rise residential estates.

S$699,897 Aug 2025 – Feb 2028 PI: A/Professor Lau Siu-Kit Eddie
MOE – ERFP

Shaping Learning Spaces with Acoustical Systems in Singapore's Multilingual Education

Investigating how acoustical design of classroom environments affects speech intelligibility and learning outcomes across Singapore's multilingual student population.

S$449,995 Jan 2025 – Jan 2028
NRF / MND / HDB – City of Tomorrow

Modelling and Development of Poro-Metamaterials for Inter-Floor Noise Isolation

Designing and testing novel poro-metamaterial systems for attenuating inter-floor impact noise in HDB high-rise housing, with prototyping and test-bed validation in a live residential setting.

S$1,999,885 Pending (3-year project, shortlisted & approved)
